title: Calawaya affinis
intro: Calawaya affinis, also known as Andean mint or Calamint, is a flowering plant native to the Andean region, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The bioactive constituents of Calawaya affinis include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, tea, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as creams and ointments, to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
